title: Easy Means of Enabling Swap Memory on Podman Machine (MacOS, maybe others)
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

I'm wondering if there is an easy way to enable / set swap memory on the podman machine, similar to how memory can be set during the init by using the `-m` flag.

IE Being able to do something like this:
```shell
podman machine init -m 4096 --swap 6000
```

Is there a reason swap is disabled on the underlying VM by default?

comments.body:
Something like this (swap file) could work, as a workaround:

https://github.com/coreos/docs/blob/master/os/adding-swap.md
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@ashley-cui Any movement on this?
@afbjorklund @jamesmikesell interested in opening a PR?
No movement at the moment, going to unassign for now and pick it up later if no one else has

@afbjorklund  i had something similar in my notes on how to add swap by ssh-ing into the vm... however something simpler would certainly be welcome.

Here are my notes:

```shell
podman machine ssh

sudo dd if=/dev/zero of=/var/swap-file bs=1024 count=6000000
sudo chmod 600 /var/swap-file
sudo mkswap /var/swap-file
sudo swapon /var/swap-file
echo "/var/swap-file none swap sw 0 0" | sudo tee -a /etc/fstab
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@benoitf Is this something we should add to Podman-Desktop?
@rhatdan Podman Desktop could call/mnage the command but IMHO flag should be available through podman machine cli as well.

I agree that it makes sense to provide this for  mac & linux. On Windows things are different since they are tuned automatically and are global to all other running WSL distros and podman machine instances. IMO best would be to treat those values on Win as a noop like we do with the m flag.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jamesmikesell Interested in opening a PR for this?

@rhatdan I don't have any modifications to the podman code base, and as such can't do a PR.  My notes above were just commands that had to be manually executed against a running podman machine instance.

Those commands would be useful to add potentially to a ignition script potentially.

You don't need modifications rights to the podman code, anyone can open a PR.
fcos offers a zram swap mechanism.  would that be sufficient for people?
I posted a WIP PR that allows for enabling zram in our fcos image, I think that's a good direction to go. If needed we could add regular old swap in a follow up.
